recalls.Description:
Prismatic 3D Prints has issued a recall for their Book Nooks with Lights, which are decorative items designed to fit between books on shelves. These book nooks come in three distinct designs: Fantasy Window, Gothic Cathedral Window, and a set of three Gothic Cathedral Windows. They are available in various frame colors including antique gold, pewter, black granite, white marble, and black, with window colors such as purple, red, yellow, green, and blue. Each nook features a recess for holding spare CR2032 coin cell batteries, which poses a risk to children due to easy access.

The recalled book nooks do not comply with safety standards regarding coin batteries, as they contain a lithium coin battery that is not housed in child-resistant packaging and lacks necessary warning labels. If ingested, these batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, or even fatal outcomes. Consumers are advised to take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Prismatic 3D Prints Recalls Book Nooks Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to Prismatic 3D Prints via email at prismatic3dprints1@gmail.com with 'recall' in the subject line, or visit https://prismatic3dprints1.etsy.com for further assistance.

Products.Name: Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks with Lights

Images.Caption: Recalled Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks - examples of Fantasy Window colors
Recalled Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks – examples of Gothic Cathedral Window colors
Recalled Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks Gothic Cathedral Window (set of three)
Location of spare battery holder in base of recalled Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks Fantasy Window
Location of spare battery holder in base of recalled Prismatic 3D Prints Book Nooks Gothic Cathedral Window

Retailers.Name: Available online at Etsy.com from March 2025 to October 2025, priced between $25 and $72.

Distributors.Name: Prismatic 3D Prints LLC of Loveland, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion

Remedies.Name: Instructions for receiving a free repair kit

recalls.metaDescription: Prismatic 3D Prints recalls Book Nooks due to battery ingestion risks. Learn how to ensure safety and receive a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: RH is recalling its 48" and 60" Natural Antler Chandeliers, which were made to order between September 2021 and October 2024. These chandeliers, crafted from naturally shed elk antlers, can pose a risk if installed at an angle on a vaulted ceiling or sloped beam. The 48" chandelier stands approximately 48" tall and weighs around 40 pounds, while the 60" chandelier measures about 36" tall and weighs about 45 pounds. Each model is identified by specific SKU numbers found on the order confirmation and receipt.
recalls.Title: RH Recalls Natural Antler Chandeliers Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach RH at 833-784-1852 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT on weekdays and 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Alternatively, they can email recall@rh.com or visit https://rh.com/us/en/customer-experience/safety-recalls for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled RH 48” Natural Antler Chandelier
Recalled RH 60” Natural Antler Chandelier

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of canopy hardware components breaking and chandeliers detaching, including one report in which the chandelier fell to the floor. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at RH galleries and online at https://rh.com/us/en/ from September 2021 to October 2024, and at RH Outlets nationwide until September 2025, priced between $1,700 and $7,700.

Distributors.Name: RH US, LLC, of Corte Madera, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of chandelier detachment and impact hazard if hung at an angle.

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair to replace canopy hardware for affected chandeliers.

recalls.metaDescription: RH recalls 48" and 60" Natural Antler Chandeliers due to fall risk. Contact RH for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Persilux has issued a recall for its zebra blinds due to potential strangulation and entanglement hazards that may lead to serious injury or death, particularly for children. The affected blinds are available in various colors including beige, black, dark gray, light gray, and white, with sizes ranging from 22 to 73 inches wide and 64 to 72 inches high. Each product is labeled with 'Persilux' and 'Zebra Blinds' on the packaging, and the barcode reads 'X003FFPT63'.
recalls.Title: Recall of Persilux Zebra Blinds Due to Serious Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the blinds immediately and reach out to Persilux at recall@persiluxhome.com or visit https://recall.persiluxhome.com for further assistance. Additional details can be found on their website at https://persiluxhome.com under the 'Recall Policy' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Persilux Zebra Blinds
"Persilux and Zebra Blinds" is printed on the packaging of the recalled blinds. "X003FFPT63" is printed on the barcode label.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from June 2023 through June 2025, priced between $40 and $124

Importers.Name: Zhejiang Xidamen New Material Co. Ltd., of China, doing business as Amazon seller Persilu

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including a replacement operating cord wand and installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Persilux recalls zebra blinds due to strangulation risks. Contact for a free repair kit and safety information.

recalls.Description: Autoez has issued a recall for its bamboo shades due to significant safety concerns. These shades, available in brown and measuring between 24 to 72 inches wide and 64 to 72 inches high, pose a strangulation and entanglement hazard to children. The brand name 'Autoez' can be found on a sticker label located on the top rail of the shades. This recall is in violation of federal regulations for window coverings, highlighting the potential danger they present.
recalls.Title: Recall of Autoez Bamboo Shades Due to Serious Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled shades immediately and reach out to Autoez for a full refund. Contact Autoez via email at Autoez_recall@163.com or visit https://sites.google.com/view/recall-autoez for further instructions.

Images.Caption: Recalled Autoez Bamboo Shades

Retailers.Name: Available on Walmart.com from September 2023 through May 2025, priced between $21 and $50.

Importers.Name: Sichuan Yunduan Renjia Trading Co. Ltd., of China, doing business as Walmart seller Autoez

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords and accessible inner cords, posing a risk of serious injury or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the shades, contact Autoez for a refund, cut the operating cord, label the shades as 'Recalled', and dispose of them properly. Send a photo of the discarded shades to Autoez_recall@163.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Autoez recalls bamboo shades due to strangulation risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Leon Rading is recalling its PH-05 digital wall clocks due to a serious risk of injury or death from ingestion hazards associated with the product's remote control. The clocks are rectangular, black, and come with a nine-button remote. A yellow label on the back of the clock provides warranty contact information. The packaging also features the model number PH-05 prominently.
recalls.Title: Leon Rading Recalls PH-05 Digital Wall Clocks Due to Ingestion Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Leon Rading via email at recall@lianaocoltd.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Digital Wall Clock (front) with remote control
Recalled Digital Wall Clock (back)
"Contact for warranty! support@te-rich.com" printed on yellow label on back of clock
Back of remote control with battery removed
Product packaging showing MODEL: PH-05

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at www.Amazon.com from November 2024 to January 2025 for approximately $23.

Importers.Name: Leon Rading Co. Ltd., of Owatonna, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: The digital wall clocks do not comply with federal regulations regarding products with button cell batteries. The remote control contains a lithium coin battery that is accessible to children, posing a choking hazard. Additionally, the products lack necessary warnings as mandated by Reese's Law, which could lead to serious injuries or fatalities if the batteries are ingested.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the remote control immediately, remove the lithium coin battery, and dispose of it according to local hazardous waste guidelines. They should mark the remote with 'RECALLED' in permanent ink and email a photo of the disposed remote to recall@lianaocoltd.com to obtain a full refund. Both Leon Rading and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers. Note: Proper disposal of button cell batteries is crucial.

recalls.metaDescription: Leon Rading recalls PH-05 digital wall clocks due to ingestion hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

The shades pose a strangulation and entanglement risk, particularly to children, and do not comply with federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: LuckupShein Window Roll Up Shades Recalled for Strangulation Risks and Federal Regulation Violations

recalls.Description: Joydeco Roller Window Shades, available in various colors such as beige, light gray, and white, are being recalled due to serious safety hazards. These shades, with widths ranging from 20 to 70 inches and a height of 75 inches, pose a strangulation and entanglement risk to children. The product is in violation of federal safety regulations for window coverings and does not meet required labeling standards.
recalls.Title: Joydeco Roller Window Shades Recalled for Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Shanghai Chushuo Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd via email at amazonservice@joydeco.com or visit https://recall-joydeco.com. You can also find details on their main website at www.joydeco.com by clicking on 'Recall Notice' under 'Help & Support.'

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the cords away from children and contact the company for a full refund or a free replacement operating cord wand. recalls.metaDescription: Joydeco Roller Window Shades recalled due to strangulation hazards. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: LINKCOO has issued a recall for its corded blackout roller window shades, available in white, gray, and black, with widths from 10 to 75 inches and heights of 79 inches. The shades feature the brand name 'LINKCOO' on the label sticker located on the lower pole. These products pose serious hazards due to long operating cords that can lead to strangulation or entanglement, particularly affecting children. Additionally, they do not comply with federal regulations concerning window coverings.
recalls.Title: LINKCOO Blackout Roller Window Shades Recalled Due to Strangulation Risks and Federal Regulation Violations

recalls.Description: Allesin has issued a recall for their corded blackout roller window shades due to serious risks of strangulation and entanglement that can lead to severe injury or death, particularly to children. These shades, available in white, gray, and dark gray, come in sizes from 20 to 58 inches wide and 72 inches high, with 'Allesin' marked on the label sticker located on the headrail. The shades do not meet federal safety regulations for window coverings, posing a significant hazard to consumers.

recalls.Description: Shadeks is recalling its ShadesU Roller Window Shades, which were exclusively available on Amazon.com. These roller blinds, offered in various colors and sizes ranging from 20 to 90 inches in width and 72 inches in height, pose significant safety hazards due to their long operating cords. The brand name "ShadesU" can be found on the label sticker located on the right side of the bottom rail of the blinds.
recalls.Title: Shadeks Issues Recall for ShadesU Roller Window Shades Due to Serious Strangulation Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Shadeks at 866-267-4233 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@shadesu.com. Additional details are available at www.shadesu.com/recall.

recalls.Description:
Thy Trading Company is recalling its bamboo roll up window blinds due to significant safety concerns. These semi-sheer blinds, available in various colors including light brown, brown, and blue, pose a strangulation and entanglement hazard, particularly to children. The blinds measure approximately 24" wide by 84" high and 72" wide, and are in violation of federal window covering regulations, presenting a serious risk of injury or death.

The recalled products include several SKU numbers, each corresponding to a specific color and design. Consumers are urged to stop using these blinds immediately and follow the provided instructions for safe disposal and refund registration.
recalls.Title: Thy Trading Company Recalls Bamboo Roll Up Window Blinds Due to Strangulation Risks; Sold Exclusively at Wayfair.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Thy Trading Company at 484-831-5010 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email thytradingllc@outlook.com. More information is available at https://thytrading.com/recall.html or by visiting www.thytrading.com and selecting 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Importers.Name: Thy Trading Company, of Phoenixville, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement risks due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blinds, mark them 'Recalled', dispose of them, and submit a photo for a refund

recalls.metaDescription: Thy Trading recalls bamboo roll up window blinds due to serious strangulation hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

The shades pose a strangulation and entanglement risk to children, violating federal safety regulations for window coverings.

recalls.Description: Hallmark has issued a recall for the Keepsake Christmas Tree Ornament featuring Cad Bane from Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett. This ornament, which stands approximately 4.5 inches tall and 1.5 inches wide, is characterized by a figurine wearing a brown hat and jacket, with a green face and blue cuffs. It was packaged in a collectible white storage box with red accents, marked with the code 2199QXE3341 on the bottom.
recalls.Title: Hallmark Recalls Cad Bane Christmas Tree Ornaments Due to Mold Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Hallmark at 800-425-5627 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also email them by visiting https://care.hallmark.com/s/ or find more information at www.hallmark.com/product-recalls.

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of 16 consumer reports of the recalled tree ornaments having mold on them when they were taken out of the box. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: Hallmark Cards, of Kansas City, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Mold growth due to insufficiently dried paint on the ornament

recalls.Description: Homewerks Worldwide has announced a recall for Allen + Roth lighted dimmable LED wall mirrors, which were produced between December 2022 and August 2023. These frameless bathroom vanity mirrors feature a touch sensor for dimmable LED lighting and are available in three sizes. The models affected include Homewerks model 75-102 (Lowe's item number 784783), measuring approximately 24 inches x 30 inches; model 75-103 (Lowe's item numbers 2845089 and 1124616), measuring about 30 inches x 36 inches; and model 75-104 (Lowe's item numbers 5746472 and 1124617), measuring around 36 inches x 42 inches. Model numbers and manufacturing dates can be found on labels on the back of the mirrors, while Lowe's item numbers are listed in the instruction manual and on purchase receipts.
recalls.Title: Homewerks Worldwide Issues Recall for Allen + Roth Lighted Bathroom Mirrors Due to Potential Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled mirrors and turn off the power. For further instructions on safe removal and disposal, as well as to request a full refund or a free replacement mirror, contact Homewerks Worldwide at 866-582-2069 from 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email MirrorRecall@Homewerks.com. More information is available online at www.Homewerks.com/MirrorRecall or by visiting www.Homewerks.com and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Allen + Roth frameless dimmable LED wall mirror, model 75-102
Recalled Allen + Roth frameless dimmable LED wall mirror, model 75-103
Recalled Allen + Roth frameless dimmable LED wall mirror, model 75-104

Injuries.Name: Lowe's has received 44 reports of the mirror's glass detaching from the wall attachment base. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: Homewerks Worldwide LLC, of Lake Bluff, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury and laceration due to glass detachment from wall attachment.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mirrors, turn off the power, and contact Homewerks for removal instructions and refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Culture Kings has issued a recall for two models of their Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays, designed for showcasing sneakers in a floating manner at home. The affected models include the CXF-22, featuring a rectangular frame in black or black/white, and the CXF-24B, which has an uppercase I-shaped black frame. Both models are equipped with LED lights and may come with or without a remote control. The displays are marked with the Culture Kings logo and are specifically those that levitate two sneakers.
recalls.Title: Culture Kings Recalls Sneaker Basel Magnetic Levitation Displays Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Culture Kings at 888-520-1885 from 12 p.m. to 11 p.m. PT Sunday to Tuesday, 12 p.m. to 2 a.m. PT Wednesday to Thursday, and 2 p.m. to 10 p.m. PT on Fridays. Alternatively, they can email sneakerdisplays@realtimeresults.net or visit www.recallrtr.com/sneakerdisplays or www.culturekings.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Consumers must destroy the product by cutting it in half and provide proof of destruction by sending a photo to yalande_us2021@163.com to receive a full refund. Yalande-US will directly contact all known consumers.

recalls.metaDescription: JURLEA Rugs recalled for fire hazard due to flammability violations. Contact Yalande-US for refunds and further instructions.

recalls.Description: Foiresoft Basic Zebra Roller Blinds, available in various colors and sizes from 10 to 110 inches wide and 64 inches high, are being recalled. These blinds, identifiable by the Foiresoft label on the headrail, pose a significant strangulation risk to children due to their looped operating cords. Consumers are urged to take immediate action for safety.
recalls.Title: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds Recalled Due to Strangulation Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, reach out to Softfunch Inc. at 800-518-4391 from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, or via email at recall.foiresoft@gmail.com. Additional information is available at https://www.foiresoft.co.kr/recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds (White)
Recalled Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds (Beige)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon from December 2022 through July 2023, priced between $50 and $175.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to looped operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blinds immediately and contact Softfunch Inc. for a full refund. Proof of destruction is required, which includes sending a photo of the cut product with cut cords. Dispose of the blinds in your trash. Softfunch will reach out to known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds recalled due to strangulation hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.
